name: Darnell Washington
headline: Efficient on a budget, but the target ceiling stays capped
body:
The arrow is pointing up. Washington's snap rate climbed to 57.2% in 2025 while his target share nearly doubled year over year, from 5.4% to 8.2%, and 43 looks turned into 31 catches for 364 yards. The efficiency underneath is real: 1.43 yards per route run, a 0.316 EPA per play, a 60.5% success rate, and 7.39 yards after the catch per reception from a 6-foot-7, 264-pound frame that breaks tackles. He even drew eight red-zone targets despite playing only 13 games.

The problem is the math above him. A new four-year extension locks in his role, but Pat Freiermuth, DK Metcalf, and Michael Pittman all sit ahead in the pecking order, which keeps his weekly volume thin and one touchdown is hard to bank on. Projected for roughly 33 catches and 345 yards, he's a TE3 worth stashing only in tight-end-premium or deep formats where his per-route production can quietly pay off.
